# Glossary

Generated from `WeldAndArrow/Exposition/Glossary.lean` by `lake exe exposition_gen`. Gloss accuracy remains prose; Lean checks term uniqueness, backward `seeAlso` references, and anchor resolvability. Canonical Buddhist terms are glossed for newcomers; expert caveats live in the Disclaimers.

| Term | Kind | Definition | Checked anchors | See also |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| floor | Theory | A floor is the place where no deeper support is being claimed. No claim holds there; distinctions fuse because the claim-space degenerates, not because both sides are jointly true. | `WAA.Grid.Tier.floor` | — |
| floor silence | Theorems | At the floor no claim of the row language holds and all claims are indiscernible. The conventional register exists because the floor alone says nothing. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.no_row_claim_holds_at_floor`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.floor_claims_indiscernible`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.fitting_offer_is_actTime` | `floor` |
| genjō | Canonical | Genjō means manifestation, the way something comes forward as this case. In this system it also names the pole-class face where no share is claimed. | `WAA.AtBot` | `floor` |
| grid | Theory | A grid is a deliberately small model of who responds to what and how those responses are connected. It supplies the apparatus for welds, grades, delivery, and diagnostics without pretending to be the whole doctrine. | `WAA.Grid` | `floor` |
| axiomatic reconstruction | Theory | The project's method: a fixed Signature layer supplies the primitives and generator, Zen sayings are recovered as consequences, and doctrinal cases test the resulting reconstruction rather than supplying its premises. | `WAA.Grid`, `WAA.Grid.Distinction.ObeysSeparateFuse` | `grid` |
| act-grammar | Theory | An act-grammar is a way to parse an occurrence into roles. In this system it is the three-row pattern that lets the grid discuss dependence, manifestation, resonance, practice, and causal carrying without storing a self. | `WAA.Grid.Tier`, `WAA.Grid.Distinction` | `grid` |
| mujishō-sōe | Canonical | Mujishō-sōe means mutual dependence without own-being. In this system it is the first-row dependence face of the act-grammar. | — | `act-grammar` |
| kannō-sōe | Canonical | Kannō-sōe means responsive resonance or mutual answering. In this system it is the second-row dependence face, where a call and a response are placed together. | `WAA.Grid.MountsAt` | `act-grammar` |
| banpō susumite | Canonical | Banpō susumite means the myriad dharmas advancing. In this system it is the second-row enactment face, where what arrives is answered by a concrete response. | `WAA.Grid.Actual` | `kannō-sōe` |
| shō | Canonical | Shō means realization. In this system it is read as the non-possessive face at the floor, not as a stored attainment. | — | `floor` |
| shu | Canonical | Shu means practice. In this system it names the enacted side of the occurrence, the doing that is spent as the occurrence happens. | `WAA.RawWeld` | `act-grammar` |
| shushō-ittō | Canonical | Shushō-ittō means practice and realization as non-dual. In this system it is read as one occurrence with two faces, not two events that later get joined, and grid-side it is the per-occurrence face of effectiveness. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaPoleDeed`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaEffectiveOccurrence`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.waaPoleDeed_of_produced_terminus` | `shō`, `shu` |
| shugenjō | Theory | Shugenjō is the paper's compound for practice that manifests without a scaffold. It is treated as a proper subset of genjō, not as a synonym for all manifestation. | — | `genjō`, `shu` |
| genjōkōan | Canonical | Genjōkōan is a manifest case, not an abstract principle floating free of use. In this system it names the first-row manifestation arriving in a second-row situation. | — | `genjō`, `kannō-sōe` |
| satori | Canonical | Satori means awakening or realization in ordinary Buddhist usage. In this system it is a pole-typed dharmas-forward event and is kept distinct from shō and kenshō. | — | `genjō`, `shō` |
| call | Theory | A call is something that arrives to be answered. In the grid it is the input slot of a weld, the thing a response addresses or fails to address. | `WAA.RawWeld.call`, `WAA.Grid.RecordedUtterance.answersCall` | `grid` |
| response | Theory | A response is what a being produces when it answers a call. In the grid it is the output slot of a weld and the visible side of function. | `WAA.RawWeld.response`, `WAA.Grid.MountsAt` | `call` |
| field | Theory | A field is the impersonal web of conditioning and delivery. In this system it carries connections through time without carrying an owner or self-index. | `WAA.Grid.conditions`, `WAA.Grid.ConditionsEither` | `grid` |
| inga | Canonical | Inga means cause and effect. In this system it is read as the field's conditioning structure, not as a stored moral account. | `WAA.Grid.conditions` | `field` |
| delivery | Theory | Delivery is the fact that one occurrence reaches another as part of the field. It is checked by a directed relation, but the act that receives it still has to be actual. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.DeliveredTo`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.LandsAt` | `field` |
| delivery-regime | Theory | A delivery-regime is the whole pattern of what reaches what. In this system it is ceded as a world-fact and is never something a single act commands. | `WAA.Grid.conditions` | `delivery`, `field` |
| weld | Theory | A weld is one concrete occurrence of answering. In the grid it bundles agent, call, and response, so the act's index is made by the act rather than stored before it. | `WAA.RawWeld`, `WAA.Grid.Weld`, `WAA.Grid.index` | `call`, `response` |
| reach-back | Theory | Reach-back is a reception reading what arrived as the return of some earlier deed. In the system it is full when delivery supplies the earlier deed and vacuous when it does not. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaReachBackFull`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.deliveredTo_iff_waaReachBackFull` | `delivery`, `weld` |
| upādāna | Canonical | Upādāna ordinarily means grasping or appropriation. In this system it is the traditional word closest to reach-back, with the technical decomposition handled by the grid. | `WAA.Grid.WaaAppropriates` | `reach-back` |
| vacuity | Theory | Vacuity is a place left unfilled rather than a false claim. In the system it names the reach-back shape where appropriation occurs but delivery supplies no deed to fill the second slot. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.NotDeliveredTo` | `reach-back`, `delivery` |
| share | Theory | A share is the portion of an occurrence's drive that is claimed as self-maintaining. In the grid it is the contribution value projected from a weld. | `WAA.Grid.share`, `WAA.Grid.share_eq_grade_check` | `weld` |
| grade | Theory | A grade is an ordered display of how much share a concrete response carries. It is third-personal in form but indexical in content, because it reads this weld's placement. | `WAA.Grid.grade`, `WAA.Grid.WaaMismatchGrade`, `WAA.Grid.share_eq_grade_check` | `share`, `weld` |
| clench | Theory | Clench is self-maintenance tightening around a response. In the grid it is read through live share rather than through a stored inner state. | `WAA.Grid.HasSelfPoleIndex`, `WAA.Grid.WaaAppropriates` | `share`, `weld` |
| arrogation | Theory | Arrogation is a response claiming subjecthood for itself. In this system it is per call and per weld, never a standing rank possessed by a being. | `WAA.Grid.HasSelfPoleIndex` | `clench`, `weld` |
| self-forward | Theory | Self-forward means carrying a self toward what is met. In this system it is the delusive direction of a live weld, not a permanent condition. | — | `arrogation`, `weld` |
| function | Theory | Function is the ability to mount a response at all. The grid keeps it separate from share, so answering a call and claiming the answer are not the same fact. | `WAA.Grid.MountsAt`, `WAA.Grid.RespondsToEveryCall` | `response`, `share` |
| pole | Theory | A pole is the zero-share class in the ordering. In the grid it is represented by `AtBot`, so the system can speak of no live self-index without choosing a numerical scale. | `WAA.AtBot`, `WAA.Grid.AtPoleClass`, `WAA.Grid.terminus_iff_quietOn_univ`, `WAA.Grid.atPoleClass_iff_quietOn_univ` | `share`, `genjō` |
| stone | Theory | A stone is the zero-function edge: no response is mounted. In this system it can sit at the pole only vacuously, because there is no answering act to grade. | `WAA.Grid.Stone`, `WAA.Grid.stone_is_terminus_vacuously` | `function`, `pole` |
| solipsist | Theory | The solipsist is the opposite edge from the stone. It names the imagined case where response is all self-claim and the call has effectively vanished. | — | `function`, `share` |
| terminus | Theory | A terminus is a responder whose mounted responses lie at the pole. It answers without live share, so the system treats it differently from a stone. | `WAA.Grid.Terminus`, `WAA.Grid.LiveTerminus`, `WAA.Grid.ResponsiveTerminus` | `function`, `pole` |
| tier | Lean convention | A tier is the level at which a claim is offered or tested. Lean uses tiers to distinguish the floor from an act-time weld. | `WAA.Grid.Tier`, `WAA.Grid.Tier.hasLiveShare` | `floor`, `weld` |
| Row 2 | Theory | Row 2 is the resonance row, where call and response are placed together. It is where the system states grade without turning grade into something possessed. | `WAA.Grid.Tier.hasLiveShare` | `kannō-sōe`, `grade` |
| determination clause | Theory | The determination clause says the grade is fixed by what actually drove this response. It rejects a private glow or later outcome as the source of grading. | `WAA.Grid.share_eq_grade_check` | `grade`, `response` |
| probe | Theory | A probe is a third-person way of varying calls to display a response pattern. It can show a placement, but it does not create the placement it shows. | `WAA.Grid.ProbeConstant` | `call`, `grade` |
| effectiveness | Theory | Effectiveness is a landing rate within a delivery-regime. In this system it grades whether calls land; it does not type what a responder is. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.HasShareDropLanding`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.ShortfallClosedAt` | `delivery-regime`, `grade` |
| fixed/adaptive | Theory | Fixed and adaptive describe how a responder's output relates to calls. The grid can model both without making adaptivity the ground of liberation. | `WAA.Grid.ResponseInvariant`, `WAA.Grid.ResponseVariesWithCall` | `call`, `response`, `effectiveness` |
| orthogonality rule | Theory | The orthogonality rule says two dimensions do not determine each other. Here function and share type the responder, while effectiveness grades landing. | `WAA.OrthogonalityNegative.waaEffectiveTerminus_stronger_than_terminus` | `function`, `share`, `effectiveness` |
| domain joint | Theory | The domain joint is the question of what counts as responding at all. The grid answers it by drive-composition and accepts the resulting artifact cases openly. | `WAA.Grid.MountsAt` | `function`, `response` |
| aimed call | Theory | An aimed call is a deed configured for a landing. In Lean this thin reading is just delivery from the deed to the reception, not a new intention primitive. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaAimedAt` | `call`, `delivery` |
| re-pitch | Theory | Re-pitch is what a reception hands forward as the next carried tendency. In the current signature it records the received weld's share and forgets the previous configuration. | `WAA.Grid.rePitch`, `WAA.Grid.rePitch_forgets` | `share`, `weld` |
| seed | Theory | A seed is a carried tendency rather than a bearer. In Lean it is modeled by a configuration value with no stored owner, weld, or self-index. | `WAA.Config`, `WAA.Config.tendency`, `WAA.Grid.relabel_rePitch` | `field`, `re-pitch` |
| standing/dated | Theory | Standing means carried as a tendency; dated means enacted now. The system treats reading a dated occurrence directly from a standing tendency as a diagnostic error. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.RowTag.standingDated`, `WAA.standing_does_not_determine_dated` | `seed`, `weld` |
| three registers | Theory | The three registers are carried field facts, spent weld facts, and per-call grade statements. The split keeps the system from storing self-indexed ownership between acts. | `WAA.SortedFact.register` | `field`, `weld`, `grade` |
| separate/fuse rule | Theory | The separate/fuse rule says live act-time distinctions must be kept apart and floor-level distinctions must fuse. It is the grammar behind the taxonomy's collapse and freeze diagnoses. | `WAA.Grid.Distinction.ObeysSeparateFuse` | `floor`, `tier` |
| collapse/freeze | Theory | Collapse fuses what should be separate; freeze holds separate what should fuse. These are the two basic ways the taxonomy catches a misuse of a distinction. | `WAA.Grid.Distinction.Collapse`, `WAA.Grid.Distinction.Freeze` | `separate/fuse rule` |
| assert/display | Lean convention | Assert and display are two voices for a verdict. The grid asserts grammatical failures inside its lens and only displays value-laden or soteriological shortfall. | `WAA.Grid.VerdictVoice`, `WAA.Grid.ErrorGrade`, `WAA.Grid.ErrorGrade.voice` | `collapse/freeze` |
| orange / eat this | Theory | The orange is the paper's image for its own offering. It gives a thing to receive, while the phrase eat this marks the risk of turning a description into an injunction. | — | `assert/display`, `call` |
| object-axis/subject-axis | Theory | Object-axis means being available as something received; subject-axis means mounting responses oneself. The grid keeps these axes apart, especially for stones and termini. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.ObjectAxisStanding`, `WAA.Grid.MountsAt` | `delivery`, `function` |
| index-question | Theory | The index-question asks whose act or whose fruit is at issue. In this system the answer is enacted in a weld or stated by a grade, never stored in the field. | `WAA.Grid.index`, `WAA.Grid.HasSelfPoleIndex` | `weld`, `field`, `grade` |
| mis-feed | Identification | A mis-feed is an internal diagnostic where the wrong register is fed to a question. The checked fence covers index-free field answers being used where an index-bearing designation is required. | `WAA.MisFeedNegative.fence_and_gate` | `index-question`, `field` |
| upāya/provisional reading | Canonical | Upāya names an apt conventional call at the tier a being occupies. In this system a provisional reading keeps that conventional function while the floor re-empties its absolutization. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.Metaphysics.provisional_preserved`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.waaPathOught_conditional` | `tier`, `separate/fuse rule`, `assert/display` |
| dukkha | Canonical | Dukkha ordinarily names suffering or unsatisfactoriness. In this system it is the mismatch of a live self-maintaining response with delivery that answers to no self. | `WAA.Grid.WaaMismatchGrade`, `WAA.Grid.WaaMismatchLive` | `share`, `delivery`, `clench` |
| before/after | Theory | Before and after are ordinary temporal words. In this system they are a reading over roles in the field, not extra furniture stored inside the field. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.TimeDirection` | `field`, `delivery` |
| arrow | Theory | The arrow is direction read into conditioning. The retyped system treats direction as a display over field structure, not as a primitive property of that structure. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.TimeDirection`, `WAA.DirectionNegative.no_direction_recovery_from_conditionsEither` | `field`, `before/after` |
| ratchet | Theory | A ratchet is a mechanism that reads a gradient as direction. In the paper it motivates why beings may read an arrow without making the arrow part of the field itself. | — | `arrow`, `field` |
| Waa marker | Lean convention | The Waa marker flags a system-point-of-view reading. It tells readers that a Lean name reports how the grid reads a concept, not what the tradition must mean by it. | `WAA.Grid.WaaAppropriates`, `WAA.Grid.WaaMismatchGrade`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaReachBackFull` | `grid` |
| DirectedConvention | Lean convention | DirectedConvention is the namespace for vocabulary that reads direction into the grid. It keeps directed delivery words separate from the direction-free signature beneath them. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.DeliveredTo` | `arrow`, `delivery` |
| being-convention | Theory | A being-convention is a diagnosis-time way of reading fine tags as macro beings. It permits naming without claiming that the grid stores the true partition. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening` | `grid`, `DirectedConvention` |
| coarsening | Lean convention | A coarsening maps fine tags to macro tags. Lean uses it to model a chosen being-reading while leaving the underlying grid unchanged. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.InFiber` | `being-convention` |
| tag/fiber | Theory | A tag is a fine identifier; a fiber is the set of fine tags read as one macro tag by a coarsening. Fiber-level predicates let the system discuss beings without storing beings as substances. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.InFiber`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.SameFiber` | `coarsening` |
| door | Lean convention | A door is a supplied diagnosis of a fine weld as body, speech, or mind. The grid does not recover this classification, and totality over the canonical three doors is an explicit modeling claim. | `WAA.Door`, `WAA.Grid.DoorReading`, `WAA.DoorsNegative.no_door_boundary_recovery` | `weld` |
| three doors | Canonical | The three doors are body, speech, and mind. Canonical arhat display is fine-being quietness through all three; a regional śrāvaka-arhat may still carry body-door residue. | `WAA.Grid.DoorQuiet`, `WAA.Grid.arhat_iff_three_doors_quiet`, `WAA.Grid.terminus_iff_quietOn_univ`, `WAA.Grid.atPoleClass_iff_quietOn_univ` | `door`, `pole` |
| voicing | Lean convention | Voicing is a supplied claim-production reading on a weld. It is intentionally door-neutral: speech, thought, and expressive bodily action can be represented, while testimonial predicates impose the speech boundary. | `WAA.Grid.SpeechReading`, `WAA.DoorsNegative.no_voicing_recovery` | `door`, `weld` |
| produced utterance | Lean convention | A produced utterance ties content to an actual voicing weld. Only a speech-door production can become a testimonial record, offered definitionally at that weld's own act-time. | `WAA.Grid.ProducedUtterance`, `WAA.Grid.ProducedUtterance.toRecorded`, `WAA.DoorsNegative.no_production_recovery` | `voicing`, `weld` |
| defiled falsehood | Theorems | Defiled falsehood is a false speech-door production at its own act-time with a live self-pole. Equating this derivable schema with deliberate lying is a separate modeling claim. | `WAA.Grid.WaaDefiledFalsehood`, `WAA.Grid.no_defiledFalsehood_of_speechDoorQuiet`, `WAA.FoxCase.oldMan_defiledFalsehood` | `produced utterance`, `pole` |
| vāsanā | Canonical | Vāsanā means residual habit or trace. The checked share-vāsanā form is live share in the body door while speech and mind are quiet; response-form habit at pole share remains future work. | `WAA.Grid.WaaVasana`, `WAA.Grid.sravakaArhat_not_arhat_witness` | `clench`, `three doors` |
| śrāvaka-arhat | Canonical | The door-typed śrāvaka-arhat is quiet at speech and mind. Unlike canonical three-door arhat display, this regional figure can retain a live body-door vāsanā. | `WAA.Grid.WaaSravakaArhat`, `WAA.Grid.sravakaArhat_no_defiledFalsehood`, `WAA.Grid.sravakaArhat_not_arhat_witness` | `three doors`, `vāsanā` |
| sentient/insentient tag | Theory | A sentient tag is one whose fiber mounts response somewhere; an insentient tag lacks that. The distinction is fiber-level and convention-relative. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.SentientTag`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.not_sentientTag_iff_fiber_all_stone` | `tag/fiber`, `function` |
| FiberAtPole / SelfAptTag / Patchy | Lean convention | These names describe a fiber spectrum: all actual welds at pole, all actual welds self-apt, or an irreducibly mixed case. They deliberately avoid defining an aggregate share for a fiber. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.FiberAtPole`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.SelfAptTag`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.BeingCoarsening.Patchy` | `tag/fiber`, `pole` |
| coherence (grade) | Theory | Coherence is a display grade over a coarsening. It can report how well a reading behaves, but it never licenses or forbids the naming of a being. | — | `coarsening`, `grade` |
| delegation | Theory | Delegation reads macro agency through an actual fine weld in a fiber. The share remains the fine weld's share, so macro agency does not create a new stored subject. | — | `weld`, `tag/fiber`, `share` |
| freedom witness | Theory | A freedom witness is a countermodel showing that a supposed boundary is not forced by grid data. The being-boundary version permits both split and merge readings of the same fine grid. | `WAA.BeingNegative.no_partition_recovery` | `being-convention`, `coarsening` |
| kenshō | Canonical | Kenshō ordinarily means seeing nature. In this system it is kept as a countable share-ceding event, not as a possession or stored attainment. | `WAA.Grid.IsShareDrop` | `share`, `re-pitch` |
| call/response | Theory | Call/response is the smallest public shape of an occurrence: something arrives and something answers. In the grid this pair is carried by the weld and later inspected by the taxonomy. | `WAA.RawWeld.call`, `WAA.RawWeld.response`, `WAA.Grid.RecordedUtterance` | `call`, `response`, `weld` |
| delivery-engineering | Theorems | Delivery-engineering is legal work on what may arrive. Responder-side it finds calls that land; sowing-side it configures aimed calls without commanding the regime. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaAimedAt`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.HasShareDropLanding` | `delivery`, `aimed call` |
| delivery-arrogation | Theorems | Delivery-arrogation is claiming authority over what arrives next. The system treats that as grasping the one register no weld owns. | `WAA.DeliveryArrogationNegative.command_utterance_not_fits` | `delivery-regime`, `arrogation` |
| buddha-side shortfall | Theorems | Buddha-side shortfall is failing to meet a not-yet-buddha's call where it is. It is the pole's live grade and is tracked by effectiveness rather than by terminus typing alone. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.ShortfallClosedAt`, `WAA.OrthogonalityNegative.waaEffectiveTerminus_stronger_than_terminus` | `effectiveness`, `terminus` |
| effective terminus | Theorems | An effective terminus is a responsive terminus whose own delivered deeds close every live shortfall. It removes the afflictive-obscuration face but does not by itself warrant trust in attributed sentences. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaEffectiveTerminus`, `WAA.EffectiveTerminusNegative.no_effectiveTerminus_recovery_from_run` | `buddha-side shortfall`, `assert/display` |
| no-nescience | Theorems | No-nescience is positive own-act-time truth for every pole-share speech-or-mind production by the being. It strengthens the old speech-only character check and marks the cognitive-obscuration boundary beyond arhat quiet. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaNoNescience`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.noDelusion_of_noNescience_of_terminus`, `WAA.FaithNegative.arhat_retains_nescience_witness` | `effective terminus`, `produced utterance` |
| standing enlightenment | Theorems | Standing full enlightenment is the two-obscurations faith-object: effective termination together with positive own-act-time truth for every pole-share speech-or-mind production. Silent models may voice no thought or true thought; testimony remains speech-only. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaFullyEnlightened`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaNoNescience`, `WAA.FaithNegative.noNescience_strictly_stronger_witness`, `WAA.FaithNegative.Sealed.silent_buddha_models` | `effective terminus`, `no-nescience` |
| enacted effectiveness | Theorems | Enacted effectiveness is the non-vacuous effective-terminus display plus at least one per-occurrence effective landing. It fences sealed-regime vacuity from earned occurrence and supplies the deed component of enacted full enlightenment. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaEffectivenessEnacted`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.not_effectivenessEnacted_of_undelivered` | `effective terminus`, `shushō-ittō` |
| enacted full enlightenment | Theorems | Enacted full enlightenment is the samyaksambuddha rung: the standing two-obscurations bundle together with a delivered effective occurrence and an actual faithful, fitting speech production at its own act-time. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaFullyEnlightenedEnacted`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaFaithfulSpeechOccurrence` | `standing enlightenment`, `enacted effectiveness` |
| backsliding theorem | Theorems | The backsliding theorem says a share-drop event cannot be held as a stored attainment. Later live welds are possible because nothing self-indexed is carried between acts. | `WAA.backsliding_witness`, `WAA.backsliding_rePitchSequence_witness` | `kenshō`, `re-pitch`, `seed` |
| memory theorem | Theorems | The memory theorem reads a trace as a seed and remembering as a fresh reception. Felt mineness is predicted at recall-time without being stored before recall. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.MemoryWitness.memory_witness`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaVacuousOwnershipFace`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaDiachronicWhose` | `seed`, `reach-back` |
| retrospective soul | Theorems | The retrospective soul is the error of freezing reach-back into a standing backward owner. It is the psychological version of treating fresh mineness as stored mineness. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.sowingReapingRow_not_freeze`, `WAA.no_agent_recovery_of_field_collision` | `memory theorem`, `reach-back`, `standing/dated` |
| prudence theorem | Theorems | The prudence theorem says special authority for self-concern is not recoverable from the grid. The standing cross-gap whose that prudence would need is exactly what the typing declines. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.PrudentialPrivilegeNegative.not_prudentialPrivilege` | `index-question`, `standing/dated`, `upāya/provisional reading` |
| deliberator theorem | Theorems | The deliberator theorem blocks a maximizer at three joints: supplied being-convention, absent accumulator, and failed command over delivery. It is a check on consequentialist reuse of the grid. | `WAA.ObjectiveNegative.no_grid_data_objective_for_my_drops`, `WAA.TransferNegative.adaptive_track_record_underdetermines_new_effect`, `WAA.DeliveryArrogationNegative.command_utterance_not_fits`, `WAA.Grid.ConsequentialistConvention.map_dropCount`, `WAA.Grid.ConsequentialistConvention.map_dropCountInFiber` | `being-convention`, `re-pitch`, `delivery-arrogation` |
| covariation | Theorems | Covariation means two quantities move together without being declared identical in every respect. The dukkha reading covaries with share, ordinally and per call. | `WAA.Grid.WaaMismatchGrade` | `dukkha`, `share` |
| proneness/occurrence | Theorems | Proneness is a carried tendency; occurrence is the live event. The dukkha split uses this standing/dated distinction to avoid storing suffering as a bearer. | — | `dukkha`, `seed`, `standing/dated` |
| transposition | Theorems | Transposition is the answer to the terminus question: the self-pole index disappears, while reception-side activity for others can still run. Nothing travels from one pole to another. | `WAA.Grid.no_self_pole_index_of_atBot`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.transposition_erased_downward_collapse_self_refuting` | `terminus`, `pole`, `object-axis/subject-axis` |
| kiriya / ahosi | Canonical | Kiriya and ahosi name action and lapsed fruit in Buddhist idiom around the arhat. In this system the point is derived from share-zero: fruit can land without a live index-question. | — | `share`, `pole`, `delivery` |
| mirror | Canonical | The mirror is the image of responsive output without possession. In this system it is function without share, legal as a display over a run. | `WAA.Grid.LiveTerminus` | `function`, `share`, `terminus` |
| device / device-nature | Theorems | A device is a compressed way to speak of pole-like responsiveness. It is legal as display and becomes a freeze when treated as a standing nature owned by a being. | — | `mirror`, `terminus`, `collapse/freeze` |
| maximally adaptive call | Theorems | The maximally adaptive call is the limiting case of call-fitting. The system deflates it from the ground of the terminus to the limit where buddha-side shortfall closes. | — | `fixed/adaptive`, `buddha-side shortfall` |
| generator | Theorems | The generator is the taxonomy engine that makes rows from distinctions. Its discipline is that errors must land in existing cells, force a new cell, be declined, or force a retype. | `WAA.Grid.GeneratorOutcome`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.tableOrder` | `collapse/freeze` |
| curated catalogue | Theorems | The curated catalogue is the Consequences-layer list of taxonomy targets — rows, compound positions, and content rows — against which the fixed Signature generator is run. It may grow as new collapse/freeze targets are identified without changing the axioms. | `WAA.Grid.Distinction.ObeysSeparateFuse`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.CompoundPosition` | `generator` |
| consistency vs exclusivity | Theorems | Lean establishes internal consistency and derivability relative to the fixed primitives, not truth or uniqueness. Countermodel-gated consequences show that this reconstruction coheres; they do not show that no rival reconstruction can cohere. | — | `axiomatic reconstruction`, `curated catalogue` |
| four verdicts | Theorems | The four verdicts are existing-cell, new-cell, declined, and retype. They are the public outcomes available when the generator meets a candidate error. | `WAA.Verdict`, `WAA.generatorRecord_newCell_count` | `generator` |
| Sunyata | Canonical | Sunyata means emptiness. In this system it is the no-final-freeze property of a distinction's re-emptying ladder. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.Metaphysics.Sunyata` | `collapse/freeze` |
| direction-underdetermination witness | Theorems | This witness shows that symmetric field residue does not determine direction. Two grids can agree on the undirected relation and disagree on the directed one. | `WAA.DirectionNegative.no_direction_recovery_from_conditionsEither` | `arrow`, `field` |
| being-boundary witness | Theorems | This witness shows that grid data do not recover a unique being-boundary. Merge and split coarsenings can both be legal over the same fine grid. | `WAA.BeingNegative.no_partition_recovery` | `being-convention`, `freedom witness` |
| weld-boundary witness | Theorems | Grid data do not recover a unique weld-segmentation. Merge and split readings of what counts as one call-response pairing are both legal over the same field. | `WAA.WeldNegative.no_weld_boundary_recovery` | `weld`, `being-convention`, `freedom witness` |
| intra-weld arrow row | Theorems | The intra-weld arrow row checks the call-to-response direction inside a weld as a convention layer. Its content form denies response-variation, and its witness says the unordered residue does not recover an interior arrow. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.intraWeldArrowRow`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.Metaphysics.intraWeldArrow_sunyata`, `WAA.InteriorDirectionNegative.no_interior_direction_recovery` | `arrow`, `weld`, `weld-boundary witness` |
| doer/deed row | Theorems | The doer/deed row is the schema-only MMK 8 cell: a prior-doer reading is diagnosed without adding priority as a primitive field of the grid. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.doerDeedRow`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.Metaphysics.doerDeed_sunyata`, `WAA.DoerDeedNegative.no_priority_recovery` | `weld`, `collapse/freeze`, `intra-weld arrow row` |
| coming-from / going-to | Theory | Coming-from and going-to are display labels for the two orientation readings of a weld's faces. Transposition reverses the labels; they are not tier names. | `WAA.InteriorDirectionNegative.transpose_swaps_readings`, `WAA.InteriorDirectionNegative.no_interior_direction_recovery` | `arrow`, `weld`, `intra-weld arrow row` |
| reflexivity witness | Theorems | The reflexivity witness instantiates a grid's being carrier with rung labels, showing that the beings ladder still has no final freeze when read over its own indices. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.ladderRungGrid_beings_sunyata` | `being-convention`, `Sunyata` |
| weld row / weld śūnyatā | Theorems | The weld row names the weld-grain distinction as a convention layer, and weld śūnyatā says its re-emptying ladder has no final frozen level. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.weldRow`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.Metaphysics.weld_sunyata` | `collapse/freeze`, `Sunyata` |
| declined case | Theorems | A declined case is a candidate that the taxonomy refuses to mark as error. The deaf-blind example matters because over-generating error would itself freeze the lens. | `WAA.InstructiveAbsence.declinedCase` | `four verdicts` |
| Grade 1 / Grade 2 | Theorems | Grade 1 names grammatical errors assertable inside the lens; Grade 2 names shortfalls only displayable by the lens. The difference prevents the grid from issuing its own value command. | `WAA.Grid.ErrorGrade`, `WAA.Grid.ErrorGrade.voice` | `assert/display` |
| clenched reception | Theorems | A clenched reception is an arriving response taken tightly as mine. It is still graded as a fresh weld, not as evidence of a stored owner. | — | `clench`, `reach-back`, `weld` |
| declining the orange | Theorems | Declining the orange is a low-resonance reception of this offered call. It is not a global wrong and does not prove anything about a person. | — | `orange / eat this`, `call` |
| defiance | Theorems | Defiance is arrogation hardened into policy. The fighting stance is a seed, but each fight remains a new act. | — | `arrogation`, `seed`, `weld` |
| cetanā | Canonical | Cetanā means intention or volition. In this system it is the traditional anchor for the claim that grading tracks the weld rather than the downstream event. | `WAA.Grid.grade_independent_of_conditions`, `WAA.Grid.share_independent_of_conditions` | `weld`, `grade` |
| cetanā theorem | Theorems | The cetanā theorem is the derived version of intention as karma. It says the grading component follows the weld even where event-success and delivery diverge. | `WAA.Grid.grade_independent_of_conditions`, `WAA.Grid.share_independent_of_conditions`, `WAA.cetana_grading_tracks_weld_not_field_witness`, `WAA.cetana_live_share_without_object_standing_witness` | `cetanā`, `weld`, `field` |
| futility theorem | Theorems | The futility theorem says the deliberate attempt fails at target and downstream. The body can be staticized as a delivery loss without subtracting object-axis standing. | `WAA.Grid.staticized`, `WAA.Grid.futility_delivery_loss_real`, `WAA.Grid.futility_object_axis_subtraction_nil`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.staticized_objectAxisStanding_iff` | `delivery`, `object-axis/subject-axis`, `device / device-nature` |
| victim-rank | Theorems | Victim-rank is severity read from the victim's station. The system treats it as a freeze because rank is being smuggled into what should be per-weld grading. | — | `grade`, `collapse/freeze` |
| hell-typed | Theorems | Hell-typed means a configuration receives no gentler call in the actual delivery-regime. The claim is regime-relative, not a statement about all possible calls. | — | `delivery-regime`, `call` |
| ledger | Theorems | A ledger is a public register that prices what can be counted. In the Huichang case it names an economic modality and the errors produced by reading that modality as a being-sized truth. | `WAA.LedgerCase.decree_engineers_calls_not_receptions`, `WAA.LedgerCase.purge_loop_runs_on` | `delivery-regime`, `Grade 1 / Grade 2` |
| economic legibility | Theorems | Economic legibility is a field-fact about which configurations a purge can reach. The system can assert that register while only displaying the worth of survival. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.landing_call_in_modality` | `ledger`, `field` |
| gradeability rule | Theorems | The gradeability rule says a recorded utterance may be graded only where the record carries its call. Lean checks both the severed-transcript obstruction and the call-carrying positive form. | `WAA.GradeabilityNegative.no_grade_recovery_from_severed`, `WAA.GradeabilityNegative.gradeability_severed_underdetermination_witness`, `WAA.recordedUtterance_grade_determined` | `call`, `grade`, `weld` |
| transcription | Theorems | Transcription turns an adaptive event into a fixed record. In the system the transcript is a seed-like fixed call, not a stored fault in the words. | — | `fixed/adaptive`, `seed`, `call` |
| per-call/global freeze | Theorems | The per-call/global freeze turns a local grade into a global rank. It is the error behind treating stages, awakening, or census categories as stored altitude. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.RowTag.perCallGlobal` | `grade`, `collapse/freeze` |
| subitism/gradualism | Canonical | Subitism and gradualism name sudden and staged arrival patterns. The system permits both as run shapes while refusing to store a rate preference as attainment. | `WAA.Grid.WaaSuddenArrival`, `WAA.Grid.WaaGradualArrival`, `WAA.Grid.rate_invisible_to_config`, `WAA.SuddenGradualNegative.subitism_frequency_underdetermined` | `re-pitch`, `per-call/global freeze` |
| PathFactor | Lean convention | PathFactor names the factor-side regrouping of the existing fetter table. Rites, view, and resolve are active; speech and conduct are named but inert until the upper-pair question is settled. | `WAA.PathFactor`, `WAA.PathFactor.blockerClass` | `coarsening`, `tag/fiber` |
| FactorHeld | Lean convention | FactorHeld is a seen-run witness: an actual in-fiber weld in the factor blocker class carries a live self-pole index. It is not itself an error; it is factor-frame vocabulary, not freeze/collapse and not clench alone. | `WAA.Grid.FactorHeld` | `PathFactor`, `clench`, `weld` |
| FactorReleased | Lean convention | FactorReleased is the release half of the factor pair: the fiber is at pole on the factor blocker class. One actual live weld in that class refutes it. | `WAA.Grid.FactorReleased`, `WAA.Grid.not_factorHeld_of_factorReleased` | `PathFactor`, `FactorHeld`, `pole` |
| WaaStreamEnterer | Lean convention | WaaStreamEnterer is the path-position reading over the first pair: rites released, view held on the seen run. It deliberately separates path display from the fruit cut. | `WAA.Grid.WaaStreamEnterer` | `FactorHeld`, `FactorReleased` |
| WaaStreamWinner | Lean convention | WaaStreamWinner is the fruit-position reading for stream entry: rites and view are released, and Lean proves it coincides with the existing stream-entry cut class. The terminology is a checked departure from treating sotapanna as only one undivided label. | `WAA.Grid.WaaStreamWinner`, `WAA.Grid.waaStreamWinner_iff_streamEntry_cutClasses` | `WaaStreamEnterer`, `FactorReleased` |
| WaaOnceReturner | Lean convention | WaaOnceReturner keeps the stream-winning fruit and adds a witnessed resolve hold on the seen run. Its extra checked content is attenuation, not a new cut class. | `WAA.Grid.WaaOnceReturner` | `WaaStreamWinner`, `FactorHeld` |
| WaaNonReturner | Lean convention | WaaNonReturner is the fruit-position reading after resolve release. Lean proves it is exactly the non-return cut class, so the factor scheme remains a reading over the fetter lattice. | `WAA.Grid.WaaNonReturner`, `WAA.Grid.waaNonReturner_iff_nonReturn_cut` | `WaaOnceReturner`, `FactorReleased` |
| WaaResolveAttenuation | Lean convention | WaaResolveAttenuation gives once-return positive content: a strict resolve-class share-drop run whose final tendency has not reached the pole. | `WAA.Grid.WaaResolveAttenuation`, `WAA.Grid.waaOnceReturner_attenuation_witness`, `WAA.Grid.registerResolve_not_released` | `WaaOnceReturner`, `re-pitch` |
| WaaSerialFactorRegime | Lean convention | WaaSerialFactorRegime is the conditional voice for factor order. If the supplied regime reads seen share-drops as rites before view before resolve, it promotes path readings to fruit readings; the grid never derives the regime or any frequency claim. | `WAA.Grid.WaaSerialFactorRegime`, `WAA.Grid.waaSerialFactorRegime_conditional`, `WAA.FactorsNegative.factor_order_underdetermined` | `WaaResolveAttenuation`, `subitism/gradualism` |
| śraddhā | Canonical | Śraddhā means faith or trust. In this system it is modeled as an antecedent for testimony and path response, never as a field fact the grid can discharge by itself. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.Factive`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaNoNescience`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.waaFaithOught_conditional` | `buddha-side shortfall`, `delivery` |
| icchantika | Canonical | Icchantika ordinarily names a being cut off from buddhahood. Here it is the terminus's inverse: non-stone, with a live self-pole index at every mounted response. It is reachable as a receiver and cannot be seated as an enlightened agent on its run. The permanent cannot-become-buddha verdict is declined, because foreclosure would be a stored rank the system forbids; defiance is a seed, not a rank. | `WAA.Icchantika`, `WAA.not_waaEffectiveTerminus_of_icchantika`, `WAA.aversionContext_of_icchantika_reception`, `WAA.icchantika_release_not_foreclosed` | `defiance`, `terminus`, `backsliding theorem`, `śraddhā` |
| tariki / other-power | Canonical | Tariki means other-power. In this system it is a delivery-regime reading, not a second act-grammar: the reception is still an ordinary weld. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaTarikiLine`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.reception_typing_ignores_sower`, `WAA.TarikiCase.universal_fixed_call_lands_without_reading`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.regime_does_not_determine_share` | `delivery-regime`, `weld` |
| pariṇāmanā / dedication | Canonical | Pariṇāmanā means dedication or turning over merit. In this system it is sowing-side share-cession whose routing is left to delivery rather than commanded by the act. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.CompoundPosition.ledgerPicture`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.CompoundPosition.ledgerPicture_decomposition`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.BeingConvention.GridConvention.CompoundPosition.ledgerPicture_contains_legal_causalSkeleton`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.SameAgentDelivery`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.CrossAgentDelivery`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.reception_typing_ignores_sower`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.regime_does_not_determine_share`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.share_does_not_determine_regime` | `share`, `delivery-regime`, `aimed call` |
| instructive absences | Theorems | Instructive absences are gaps the system keeps because they do diagnostic work. Lean mirrors them as data with status and numbering rather than treating them as forgotten cases. | `WAA.InstructiveAbsence`, `WAA.InstructiveAbsence.status`, `WAA.InstructiveAbsence.number` | `declined case`, `generator` |
| ceded cosmology | Theorems | Ceded cosmology marks the rebirth boundary: the grammar of ownerless continuation is derived, but persistence across biological death, the realms, and their mechanism remain world-facts downstream of the mounted-response domain. The boundary is recorded as data, not crossed by a theorem. | `WAA.InstructiveAbsence.rebirthCosmology`, `WAA.InstructiveAbsence.rebirthCosmology_anchor` | `instructive absences` |
| identification claim | Identification | The identification claim says karma names the loop where the field carries diachronic relations and the weld enacts every index. It is a decomposition claim, not a stored-self claim. | — | `field`, `weld`, `index-question` |
| state-designation vs. act-fixing | Identification | State-designation names something over a standing base at a time; act-fixing makes the relevant index in the act itself. The contrast blocks a state-tool from ranging over karmic ownership. | `WAA.Grid.stateToolFits_iff_atBot` | `standing/dated`, `weld` |
| offices-spine | Identification | The offices-spine is the list of traditional places where karmic ownership has work to do. The identification argues that each office discharges at act-time. | `WAA.WaaOwnershipOffice` | `identification claim`, `weld` |
| thin for-me-ness | Identification | Thin for-me-ness is pre-reflective givenness without appropriation. The system keeps the token-reflexive light needed by the weld and removes standing ownership. | — | `weld`, `arrogation` |
| Sartrean occupant | Identification | The Sartrean occupant is the remaining inhabitant of clench-as-structure after thin for-me-ness is pared back. It names anguish as a structure, not ownership as a stored thing. | — | `thin for-me-ness`, `clench` |
| sower-reaps split | Identification | The sower-reaps split separates the report that a delivery line exists from the ownership face made at reception. It keeps true field reporting away from standing ownership. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaReportFace`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaOwnershipFace`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.WaaVacuousOwnershipFace` | `delivery`, `reach-back` |
| personal-karma convention | Identification | A personal-karma convention restricts delivery to same-agent lines as a legal overlay. The restriction is floor-optional: it leaves reception typing neutral and becomes a freeze only when held as necessary. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.reception_typing_ignores_sower`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.regime_does_not_determine_share`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.share_does_not_determine_regime` | `delivery-regime`, `sower-reaps split` |
| svakarma demotion | Theorems | The svakarma demotion lowers the claim that fruit ripens only in the doer's continuum from metaphysical necessity to contingent delivery-regime. It permits a personal-karma overlay without making that overlay signature law. | `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.SameAgentDelivery`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.CrossAgentDelivery`, `WAA.Grid.DirectedConvention.reception_typing_ignores_sower`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.regime_does_not_determine_share`, `WAA.OtherPowerNegative.share_does_not_determine_regime` | `pariṇāmanā / dedication`, `personal-karma convention` |
| pole-typing corollary | Identification | The pole-typing corollary says the state-tool fits exactly where live welding ceases. At share-zero there is no live self-pole index for the tool to miss. | `WAA.Grid.stateToolFits_iff_atBot`, `WAA.Grid.no_self_pole_index_of_atBot` | `pole`, `index-question` |
| soul-guard | Identification | The soul-guard blocks storage by making the agent more empty, not more substantial. Both forward-facing and retrospective bearers are declined. | `WAA.no_agent_recovery_of_field_collision` | `field`, `weld` |
| being trichotomy | Identification | The being trichotomy gives three readings: collapse deletes beings, freeze reifies a partition, and convention leaves naming free. It keeps designation useful without making it metaphysical. | `WAA.Disclaimer.beingTrichotomy` | `being-convention`, `collapse/freeze` |
| hare's horn | Canonical | A hare's horn is the stock example of something nameable but unrealizable. In this system it shows that designation is constrained at use, not at the mere act of naming. | `WAA.Disclaimer.hareHornRegister` | `being-convention` |
| Modal Realism | Canonical | Modal Realism treats possible beings as fully real. In this system it is the beings-row freeze: plenitude moved into ontology rather than left as conventional designation. | `WAA.Disclaimer.modalRealismFreeze` | `being trichotomy`, `collapse/freeze` |
| disclaimers | Identification | The disclaimers are the paper's explicit flags for original or non-canonical moves. They say which precision remains prose even when Lean checks anchors, numbering, and structural constraints. | `WAA.Disclaimer`, `WAA.Disclaimer.number` | `instructive absences` |
